name: Leapcell
description: Multi-language PaaS supporting both serverless pay-as-you-go and persistent server deployment for web frameworks.

seo.title: Leapcell — Backend-as-a-Service
seo.meta_description: Leapcell: Multi-language PaaS supporting both serverless pay-as-you-go and persistent server deployment for web frameworks.

when_to_use: Multi-language serverless PaaS with pay-as-you-go billing
Deploying Node.js, Python, Go apps serverlessly without config
Persistent server deployment alongside serverless functions
Simple Heroku alternative with per-request billing model
Rapid backend deployment for web frameworks without DevOps

pros: Supports both serverless and persistent modes
cons: Small player with limited track record
Fewer integrations than Render or Railway
review.verdict: Flexible new PaaS with interesting hybrid model, but still proving itself against Render and Fly.
review.best_for: Indie devs and small teams wanting flexible serverless plus persistent deployment options
review.not_for: Enterprises needing mature support, SLAs, or deep compliance certifications
